dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6898 IH: Airship Improvement Research for Safety and Humanitarian Innovation Projects Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Sykes (for herself and Mr. Joyce of Ohio) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology

form.official-title: To amend title 51, United States Code, to promote advancements and innovation in United States aeronautical research and technology for enhanced safety, noise, resiliency, and improved environmental impacts in United States aviation systems.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Airship Improvement Research for Safety and Humanitarian Innovation Projects Act or the AIRSHIP Act.

subsection.text: It is the sense of Congress that—

paragraph.text: airships could contribute to sustainable and clean air cargo transportation;
airships may one day find much needed applications in supporting disaster response efforts or delivering humanitarian aid to those in need and to areas that may be inaccessible; and
advances in airship design and technology could enable and reintroduce the world to practical, economical, and environmentally friendly airship transportation.

subsection.text: Section 40112 of title 51, United States Code, is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking The Administrator and inserting the following:
in paragraph (1), as so designated, by inserting , airship, after rotorcraft; and
by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: The Administrator may establish a program of competitively awarded grants available to teams of researchers that may include the participation of individuals from universities, industry, and government for the conduct of this research.

subparagraph.text: by striking The Administrator and inserting the following:
in paragraph (1), as so designated, in the first sentence, by inserting , airship, after fixed wing vehicles; and
by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: The Administrator may establish a program of competitively awarded grants available to teams of researchers that may include the participation of individuals from universities, industry, and government for the conduct of this research.
